result.markdown:
--- title: "Benchmark datasets" subtitle: "Load deterministic fixtures and score estimated betas against known truth." jupyter: python3 execute: freeze: auto --- ## Available Fixtures `fmrimod` includes small deterministic benchmark fixtures for examples and regression tests. These fixtures are numerical canaries: useful for checking algebra and examples, but distinct from flagship workflow parity reports in `benchmarks/parity/`. ::: {#24251d21 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} import numpy as np import pandas as pd import fmrimod as fm fm.list_benchmark_datasets() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ```{=html}
Dataset Description
0 BM_Canonical_HighSNR Deterministic canonical HRF benchmark fixture
``` ::: ::: ## Inspect a Dataset ::: {#df4ab65b .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} name = "BM_Canonical_HighSNR" summary = fm.get_benchmark_summary(name) summary["dimensions"], summary["experimental_design"] ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ``` ({'n_timepoints': 48, 'n_voxels': 4, 'n_events': 6, 'n_conditions': 2}, {'conditions': ['A', 'B'], 'events_per_condition': {'A': 3, 'B': 3}, 'TR': 1.5, 'total_time': 72.0, 'target_snr': 'high'}) ``` ::: ::: ::: {#444fe74a .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} data = fm.load_benchmark_dataset(name) data.keys() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ``` dict_keys(['description', 'event_onsets', 'condition_labels', 'TR', 'total_time', 'target_snr', 'true_betas_condition', 'true_hrf_parameters', 'noise_parameters', 'event_table', 'Y_noisy']) ``` ::: ::: ## Build a Design Matrix ::: {#d831770d .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} X = fm.create_design_matrix_from_benchmark(name, hrf="spmg1") X.head() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ```{=html}
Intercept A B
0 1.0 0.000000 0.0
1 1.0 0.000000 0.0
2 1.0 0.000000 0.0
3 1.0 0.016330 0.0
4 1.0 1.048172 0.0
``` ::: ::: ## Fit OLS and Score It The benchmark stores condition-level ground truth, so drop the intercept row before evaluating. This page uses direct matrix algebra to make the scoring transparent; user-facing analyses should prefer `fmri_dataset -> fmri_lm`. ::: {#1ec7ec2a .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} Y = data["Y_noisy"] beta = np.linalg.lstsq(X.to_numpy(), Y, rcond=None)[0] condition_beta = beta[1:, :] performance = fm.evaluate_method_performance(name, condition_beta, "OLS") pd.Series(performance["overall_metrics"]).round(4) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=5} ``` correlation 1.0000 mse 0.0000 rmse 0.0027 mae 0.0019 dtype: float64 ``` ::: ::: ## Compare Estimated and True Betas ::: {#31cd1029 .cell execution_count=7} ``` {.python .cell-code} true_beta = data["true_betas_condition"] comparison = pd.DataFrame({ "true": true_beta.ravel(), "estimated": condition_beta.ravel(), }) comparison.corr().iloc[0, 1].round(5) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=6} ``` 0.99999 ``` ::: ::: ## Next - [Simulation](simulation.qmd) - [Linear model](linear-model.qmd)
